lessons.title: Flutter Basics & Widgets
lessons.content: In Flutter, everything is a widget. Widgets are nested to build the UI structure. The widget tree consists of structural, stylistic, and behavioral elements.

lessons.title: Asynchronous Programming in Dart
lessons.content: Dart uses Futures and Streams to handle asynchronous work. The async and await keywords allow you to write asynchronous code that reads like synchronous code.

lessons.title: State Management with Providers
lessons.content: State management is the practice of managing changes to data within the app. Providers propagate changes down the widget tree using InheritedWidgets.

questions.question: What is the core building block of a Flutter UI?
Which tree is responsible for handling layouts and paints?
What keyword is used to pause execution until a Future completes?
